---
name: ms-presentation-deck
description: |
  Gera o deck HTML no padrão ms-identity (Microsoft 4-color, Inter + JetBrains Mono): um deck HTML trilíngue para apresentador, com painel de speaker notes e presenter view, que é o entregável principal e a fonte da verdade. Esse HTML com notas é o que o GitHub Copilot entrega por padrão quando a Paula pede um deck. Os outros três formatos (HTML público sem notas, PDF por slide, PPTX nativo editável) são derivados sob demanda, só quando pedidos explicitamente, nunca por padrão. Use SEMPRE que a Paula pedir um deck, apresentação, slides, briefing, talk, ou material visual para audiência, mesmo se mencionar só conteúdo. Use também para "atualizar o deck", e para os derivados quando pedidos: "versão pública", "exportar pra PPTX", "gerar o PDF". Dispara em PT-BR, EN, ES ou multilíngue, mesmo se o pedido começar pelo conteúdo sem dizer "deck". Não use para playbook (use ms-identity-editorial-brief), SVG solto (use svg-professional), nem decks em Gamma ou Canva.
---

# ms-presentation-deck

Engine de apresentação para a Frontier Cockpit Team (Software Global Black Belt, Microsoft). O entregável principal é **o deck HTML trilíngue com speaker notes e presenter view**. Esse HTML é a fonte da verdade e é o único arquivo gerado por padrão. Os outros três formatos (HTML público sem notas, PDF, PPTX nativo) são derivados dele sob demanda, só quando a Paula pede.

## When to use

Use this skill whenever the user wants a presentation deck. Examples that all trigger this skill:

- "Quero um deck sobre <topic>"
- "Faz uma apresentação executiva sobre <topic>"
- "Briefing técnico sobre <topic>"
- "Slides para uma talk de 60 minutos"
- "Atualizar o deck que fizemos sobre <topic>"
- "Exportar o deck pra PPTX"
- "Versão pública do deck pra publicar no site"
- "Deck para apresentar amanhã com painel de notas"

Don't use for: long-form documents (use `ms-identity-editorial-brief`), standalone diagrams (use `svg-professional`), Gamma decks, or Canva designs.

## The primary deliverable, and the derivatives

**The HTML deck with speaker notes is THE deliverable.** It is the source of truth and the only file generated by default. When the user asks for a deck, build this and only this, then stop.

| Default | File | Audience | What it is |
|---------|------|----------|------------|
| ⭐ **YES, always** | `<name>_multi.html` | Apresentadora | Trilíngue, painel de speaker notes (N), janela de presenter (F) com timer e auto-reconnect. This is the source of truth. |

**The other three are derivatives, generated ONLY when the user explicitly asks.** Never produce them by default. Never bundle them with the HTML "to be helpful." Each is derived from the multi.html on demand:

| On demand | File | Trigger phrase | How |
|-----------|------|----------------|-----|
| When asked | `<name>_<locale>_public.html` | "gera a versão pública", "versão sem notas", "pro site" | `scripts/make_public.py` |
| When asked | `decks/pdf/<DeckBase>/<DeckBase>_<locale>.pdf` | "gera o PDF", "exporta pra PDF" | `scripts/make_pdf.py` |
| When asked | `decks/pptx/<DeckBase>/<DeckBase>_{pt-BR,en,es}.pptx` | "exporta pra PPTX", "versão PowerPoint editável" | native PPTX generator + `scripts/validate_derivatives.py` |

After delivering the HTML, it is fine to mention in one line that the public HTML, PDF, and PPTX versions can be generated when needed, but do not generate them unless asked.

Filename pattern for deck deliverables: `<EnglishTopicCamelCase>_Deck_v<major>_<minor>_<patch>_<YYYY-MM-DD>_<variant>.<ext>`

Use English for filenames even when the deck UI copy is PT-BR or ES. Translate Portuguese title fragments in the filename only, for example a Portuguese "legacy modernization with agents" title becomes `LegacyModernizationWithAgents`. Remove duplicate download suffixes like `_1`, `_2`, and `_5` from the logical filename. Store the HTML in `decks/`, preview images in `decks/previews/`, loose deck-only screenshots or simulations in `decks/assets/`, deck PDFs in `decks/pdf/<DeckBase>/`, and deck PPTX files in `decks/pptx/<DeckBase>/`.

Variants: `multi` (trilíngue com notas, the default), `<locale>_public` (sem notas), `<locale>` (PDF per locale). PPTX exports are always a three-file locale package: `pt-BR`, `en`, and `es`.

## Workflow for the HTML deck (the default path)

This is what runs when the user asks for a deck. It stops at the HTML.

1. **Read the deck architecture reference** (`references/deck-architecture.md`) to understand the HTML structure: viewport 1280×720, locale switching via `data-i18n`, slide system, presenter view, animations.
2. **Plan the deck**: list of slides (target 10 to 25), one-line description of each, identify which patterns apply (cover, agenda, divider, 2-col, 3-card, 4-card, stat-pair, donut, terminal, FinOps layers, roadmap, closing). The showcase at `assets/showcase.html` is the visual catalog of these patterns.
3. **Run the slide quality pass** (`references/slide-quality.md`): assign every slide a role, pattern, density, anchor, and next move. Revise the plan if the same layout repeats, if the section has no hook/evidence/decision arc, or if there is no visual reset every 4 to 6 slides.
4. **Write the I18N JSON** (or extend an existing one): one key per content element, three locales (`en`, `pt-BR`, `es`).
5. **Build the multi.html** using patterns from `references/patterns.md`. This is the master source of truth.
6. **Write the speaker notes** (one per slide, three locales) following the structure in `references/speaker-notes.md`. The notes are the heart of this deliverable, not an afterthought.
7. **Apply the mandatory head block** from `references/head-meta.md`: the inline Microsoft favicon (never an external `favicon.svg`) and the Open Graph and Twitter social preview, with `og:locale` and a 1200x630 `preview-<locale>.png` per language.
8. **Validate**: run `scripts/audit.py deck.html --check-assets` (JS check, identity strings, em dashes (forbidden), note word counts, favicon, social preview meta, divider style, asset existence), render test, and the slide quality gate. Then walk `references/first-run-checklist.md` end to end.
9. **Save and present the HTML deliverable.** Write it to the workspace `output/` folder and surface it to the user. Stop here. Mention in one line that public HTML, PDF, and PPTX can be generated on request, but do not generate them.

## Workflow for the derivatives (only when explicitly requested)

Run the relevant one ONLY when the user asks for that specific format. Each reads the existing multi.html.

- **Public HTML** (no notes): `scripts/make_public.py`, forcing the target locale. Removes notes panel, presenter view, formatNote, broadcastState, key handlers N/F/P, and the N/F kbd hints.
- **PDF**: `scripts/make_pdf.py` via Playwright, slide by slide, each slide a 13.333x7.5" page, chrome hidden. Save the result under `decks/pdf/<DeckBase>/`.
- **PPTX package**: generate three native editable PowerPoint files, one per locale (`pt-BR`, `en`, `es`). Each file must contain the requested locale's visible slide text and speaker notes. Save all three under `decks/pptx/<DeckBase>/`. Never create a screenshot-based PPTX.

After generating a PDF or any PPTX file, run `scripts/validate_derivatives.py` against the source HTML and the derivative. For PPTX, run the gate three times, once for each locale. Do not present the PPTX package unless all three files pass.

Present each derivative as it is produced.

## Identity rules (NON-NEGOTIABLE)

These come from the user's persistent memory and must be enforced in every deck:

- **No em dashes (—) anywhere.** Use commas, periods, colons, or " · ". Audit before delivery.
- **"GitHub Copilot" always full name**, never abbreviated.
- **"Software Global Black Belt"** (no region suffix).
- **`frontier-cockpit@example.com`** as the only contact email (in the deck and in PPTX closing slide).
- **Microsoft palette**: `#F25022` red, `#7FBA00` green, `#FFB900` yellow, `#00A4EF` blue.
- **Fonts**: Inter (sans), JetBrains Mono (mono). PowerPoint exports use these names; Office falls back to similar fonts when not installed.
- **Author name**: Frontier Cockpit Team.
- **Header on every slide**: 4-square logo + "PAULA SILVA  |  SOFTWARE GLOBAL BLACK BELT".

## When to load each reference file

The detailed knowledge is split across reference files to keep this SKILL.md compact. Load them as needed:

- **`references/deck-architecture.md`**: Read FIRST. The HTML structure: viewport, slide system, I18N, locale switcher, presenter view with BroadcastChannel, formatNote, kbd-hint, animations. Required to build the HTML.
- **`references/patterns.md`**: Read SECOND. The reusable layout patterns (cover, agenda, dividers, 2-column, 3-card, 4-card, stat-pair, donut, terminal, FinOps layers, roadmap, closing) with code snippets. Required to build the slides.
- **`references/slide-quality.md`**: Read THIRD. The quality gate for visual rhythm, slide roles, density, pattern selection, section arc, and avoiding repeated boring layouts. Required before writing HTML.
- **`references/speaker-notes.md`**: Read FOURTH. The structure of speaker notes: `[ABERTURA]`, `[NÚCLEO]`, `[GANCHO PROVOCATIVO]`, `[TRANSIÇÃO]`, `[TIMING]`. Required to write the notes.
- **`references/head-meta.md`**: Read FIFTH. The mandatory `<head>`: inline Microsoft favicon and Open Graph/Twitter social preview, with per-locale `og:locale` and `preview-<locale>.png`. Apply to every deck HTML.
- **`references/first-run-checklist.md`**: Read LAST, before delivery. The end-to-end gate every deck must pass on the first run: data completeness, all three locales, notes per slide, identity strings, favicon, social meta, no fabricated metrics, audit pass, render pass, and the derivative-specific checks.
- **`references/pptx-mapping.md`**: Read ONLY when the user asks for a PPTX. How each HTML pattern maps to pptxgenjs constructs, font sizes, coordinate system, common pitfalls.

## Scripts

The `scripts/` folder contains:

- **`make_public.py`**: Derives the public HTML from the multi HTML. Removes notes panel, presenter view, formatNote, broadcastState, key handlers N/F/P, kbd hints for N/F. Forces a target locale, then re-runs `audit.py` on the output so a broken public file never ships.
- **`make_pdf.py`**: Generates the PDF via Playwright, slide by slide. Each slide becomes a 13.333x7.5" page. Hides chrome (deck-controls, kbd-hint, lang-switcher) via injected CSS. Preflights its dependencies (Playwright, Chromium, pypdf) and validates the output page count.
- **`build_native_pptx.py`**: Produces native editable PPTX derivatives from the HTML source. Use `--all-locales` to build the three-file PPTX package for a deck (`pt-BR`, `en`, `es`). It uses native PowerPoint shapes, text, and notes, not full-slide images, and runs `validate_derivatives.py` after each file. The current `make_pptx.js` remains a per-deck template/reference for bespoke high-fidelity generators.
- **`audit.py`**: Runs the identity and structure audit: em dashes, abbreviated "Copilot", missing contact email, favicon, social meta, divider style, notes coverage. Pass `--check-assets` to also verify referenced preview images exist.
- **`validate_derivatives.py`**: Validates exported PDF and PPTX against the source HTML. PDF must live under `decks/pdf/<DeckBase>/`, match slide count, be 16:9, and expose selectable text. PPTX must live under `decks/pptx/<DeckBase>/`, match slide count, keep native editable shapes, avoid full-slide screenshots, and embed speaker notes for the requested locale. Use `--allow-notes-derived-content` only for PPTX packages when the HTML visible text is incomplete but localized speaker notes are complete; never use this flag for PDF.
- **`requirements.txt`**: Python dependencies for `make_pdf.py` and `validate_derivatives.py`. Install with `pip install -r scripts/requirements.txt`, then `python -m playwright install chromium`.

## Critical lessons learned

These come from real iteration cycles. Skipping them creates broken output:

1. **`reduced_motion='no-preference'` in Playwright**. Default is 'reduce', which breaks the stagger animations and makes some slides render as blank. Always set this when capturing PDF or screenshots.
2. **I18N dot notation conflicts.** Don't nest `cover.title.line1` then also try to write `cover.title` as a string. The I18N resolver follows dot paths; max 2 levels with camelCase last.
3. **Section dividers use the Microsoft-style dark divider shown in the visual reference**: `PART` / `PARTE` label, a large Roman numeral (`I`, `II`, `III`) in the section accent color, then title and subtitle. Keep the same Roman divider in HTML and use a tested size when exporting to PPTX.
4. **Run a visual rhythm pass before writing HTML.** A deck that repeats cards, tables, or text blocks for every slide is not acceptable. Use `references/slide-quality.md` and `assets/showcase.html` as the menu of patterns.
5. **PPTX shared shadow options corrupt the second shape.** PptxGenJS mutates option objects in place. Use a `makeShadow()` factory function or inline objects.
6. **Cover-page wide titles need 44pt, not 54pt.** At 54pt with `Inter` + `LAYOUT_WIDE`, the title wraps and overlaps the subtitle.
7. **For huge digits (e.g. "100x"), use inline rich text with superscript** rather than two separate textboxes. Otherwise they collide.
8. **Never reuse the same kbd-hint text in the public version.** The public version's kbd-hint is updated in `uiBundles` to remove N/F/P references; if you only edit the static HTML it gets overwritten at locale switch.
9. **Presenter view needs heartbeat + refresh button.** Without these, if the user reloads the main deck, the presenter window keeps showing stale state forever. The pattern is documented in `references/deck-architecture.md`.

## Versioning convention

When updating a deck, bump the version:

- **Patch** (`v1_0_0` → `v1_0_1`): typo fix, single-slide text update.
- **Minor** (`v1_0_x` → `v1_1_0`): new slide, layout refinement, notes rewrite.
- **Major** (`v1_x_x` → `v2_0_0`): structural change (new sections, audience pivot, major reframing).

The filename embeds the version: `Token_Management_Agent_Design_Deck_v2_0_0_2026-05-28_multi.html`.

## Validation checklist (run on the HTML before delivering)

Run this on the multi.html before presenting it:

```bash
# 1. JS validity
node --check <(grep -oP '<script>\K.*?(?=</script>)' deck.html | head -1)

# 2. Em dash audit (should be 0)
grep -c "—" deck.html

# 3. Identity strings
grep -c "Software Global Black Belt" deck.html  # >= 1
grep -c "frontier-cockpit@example.com" deck.html    # >= 1
grep -c "GitHub Copilot" deck.html              # >= 1

# 4. Forbidden strings
grep -c "@your-org\|your-handle\|Microsoft Americas\|Frontier Cockpit\|Microsoft Global Black Belt" deck.html  # 0

# 5. Full identity, structure, and asset audit
python3 scripts/audit.py deck.html --check-assets
```

Before delivery, also run the slide-quality gate manually from `references/slide-quality.md`: every slide must have a role, pattern, density, anchor, and next move; no layout repeats three times in a row; each section must include hook, evidence, mechanism, and decision across its slides. Then walk `references/first-run-checklist.md` end to end; it is the single gate that makes the first run correct.

## Bundled assets

- **`assets/showcase.html`** ⭐: The visual pattern showcase: all 13 slide patterns rendered as a navigable trilingual deck (EN/PT/ES), plus a reference section documenting each pattern with when-to-use, anti-pattern, engagement tip, ideal density, and suggested timing, plus 6 presentation principles. Open this in a browser as the visual spec before building a deck, and use it to choose which patterns fit the content. This is the canonical catalog; build decks with this full visual vocabulary, not just plain lists.
- **`references/slide-quality.md`** ⭐: The planning and quality gate that maps content types to patterns, enforces rhythm, prevents repetitive decks, and turns the showcase into a practical slide plan.
- **`assets/template_data.json`**: Skeleton I18N structure showing what keys each slide pattern needs.
- **`assets/example_deck_*`**: A reference example (the Token Management deck: multi.html, public.html, pptx, data.json, pptx_generator.js) for the agent to look at when in doubt.
